membership_plans.name: Student
membership_plans.description: Discounted membership for students with valid ID.
benefits: Access to online resources and tutorials
Discounted pricing on workshops
Access to members-only forum areas

membership_plans.name: Basic
membership_plans.description: Core membership level for hobbyists getting started with the club.
benefits: Access to regular meetings and critiques
Member pricing on events and workshops
Access to online resource library
Eligibility to join mentorship program as mentee

membership_plans.name: Standard Annual (Exhibit)
membership_plans.description: Most popular plan for active members who want to participate in exhibits.
benefits: All Basic membership benefits
Exhibit participation in at least one annual club show
Eligibility for member spotlight features
Priority sign-up period for limited workshops
Access to member-only photo walks

membership_plans.name: Enthusiast Plus (Exhibit)
membership_plans.description: Expanded benefits for dedicated photographers who exhibit regularly.
benefits: All Standard membership benefits
Exhibit participation in up to three club shows per year
Waived submission fees for club-run contests
Early access to juried exhibit calls
Access to quarterly portfolio review sessions
One free guest pass per month to bring a friend to meetings
Access to advanced lighting and studio gear library
Priority waitlist for sold-out workshops

membership_plans.name: Premium Patron
membership_plans.description: Supporter level for members who want to invest extra in the club’s mission.
benefits: All Enthusiast Plus benefits
Name listed as patron on exhibit materials
Invitation to annual patron appreciation dinner
One complimentary multi-week course per year

mentor_profiles.name: Avery Clark
mentor_profiles.bio: Full-time wildlife photographer with over 12 years of experience documenting birds and mammals across North America. Specializes in ethical field practices and beginner-friendly instruction.

mentor_profiles.name: Jordan Singh
mentor_profiles.bio: Conservation-focused wildlife shooter who leads small-group expeditions and workshops in national parks.

mentor_profiles.name: Maria Lopez
mentor_profiles.bio: Award-winning wildlife and nature photographer, with work published in several international magazines.

mentor_profiles.name: Samuel Rivera
mentor_profiles.bio: Landscape specialist known for dramatic long exposure coastal scenes and night skies.

mentor_profiles.name: Elliot Chen
mentor_profiles.bio: Portrait and studio lighting mentor who enjoys breaking down complex lighting setups into approachable steps.

mentor_profiles.name: Taylor Nguyen
mentor_profiles.bio: Street photographer focused on candid storytelling and visual rhythm in busy urban environments.

mentor_profiles.name: Olivia Hart
mentor_profiles.bio: Macro photography enthusiast who teaches creative ways to capture tiny subjects with minimal gear.

mentor_profiles.name: Riley Brooks
mentor_profiles.bio: Generalist mentor with experience across landscapes, portraits, and events, focused on building solid fundamentals.

resource_articles.title: Beginner Camera Guide 2026: Best Options Under $600
resource_articles.summary: An up-to-date overview of beginner-friendly cameras under $600, with recommendations for different shooting styles.
resource_articles.content:
In this 2026 beginner camera guide, we compare several bodies that balance price and performance.

Recommended Cameras:
- BrightPix 200: Ideal for general use and low-light shooting.
- NovaFlex M5: Great for travel with a compact body.
- Skyline Compact 20: Perfect if you want a simple all-in-one camera.

We also explain key specs like sensor size, lens options, and autofocus systems to help you make a confident decision.

resource_articles.title: Budget Travel Kits for Beginner Photographers
resource_articles.summary: Three lightweight camera setups under $600 for travel and everyday photography.
resource_articles.content:
Travel photography rewards light and flexible gear. In this guide we highlight three main camera options and matching accessories.

Recommended Cameras:
- UrbanCapture S40: A compact zoom camera ideal for city breaks.
- MountainView D150: A DSLR kit for those who want an optical viewfinder.
- Skyline Compact 10: The simplest option, great if you want pocketable gear.

Each section includes lens suggestions, carrying tips, and packing checklists.

resource_articles.title: Intermediate APS-C Cameras Compared
resource_articles.summary: Compare the latest APS-C camera bodies for enthusiasts stepping up from beginner gear.
resource_articles.content: We compare bodies like the RiverLight Z3 and MountainView D300, focusing on autofocus, burst rates, and handling.

resource_articles.title: Understanding the Exposure Triangle
resource_articles.summary: Learn how aperture, shutter speed, and ISO work together to create a properly exposed image.
resource_articles.content: Exposure is controlled by three main variables: aperture, shutter speed, and ISO. This tutorial walks through each one with diagrams and example images.

resource_articles.title: How to Shoot Night Cityscapes Without a Tripod
resource_articles.summary: Techniques for getting sharp nighttime images handheld in the city.
resource_articles.content: Shooting at night doesn’t always require a tripod. Learn how to brace against solid objects, use image stabilization, and pick the right settings.

resource_articles.title: A Brief History of the Downtown Photo Club
resource_articles.summary: From a small meetup in a coffee shop to a thriving community organization.
resource_articles.content: The club began as a weekly coffee meetup and has grown into a 200+ member organization with exhibits, workshops, and volunteer projects.

resource_articles.title: 5 Tips to Get More Out of Photo Walks
resource_articles.summary: A short article with practical advice for improving your images during group walks.
resource_articles.content: From scouting locations to limiting your gear, these five tips will help you stay focused and creative on your next walk.

resource_articles.title: Affordable Tripods Under $150 for Landscape Photography
resource_articles.summary: We compare several sturdy yet affordable tripods suitable for landscape photographers on a budget.
resource_articles.content: A solid tripod doesn’t have to be expensive. This guide explains what to look for in tripod height, weight, load capacity, and leg locks.

resource_articles.title: Checklist for Giving Constructive Critique
resource_articles.summary: A simple checklist to help you give helpful, respectful feedback during critique nights.
resource_articles.content: Use this checklist as a framework for talking about composition, light, and storytelling when critiquing images.

courses.description: Explore framing, visual rhythm, and storytelling projects with weekly assignments and in-class critique.

courses.title: Night Photography Bootcamp (2-week Series)
